verses.text: The LORD: Aaron and sons separate from the holy things, that they profane not My name. Whoever of his seed has issue, leprosy, touches dead, unclean — and eats holy things — cut off. Bathe at evening; then eat. Not eat what dies of itself or torn. Keep My ordinance. Guidance: Holy bread is for clean hands. A priest's body has to be ready before he reaches for what God has set apart. The food is gracious, the path to it is careful.

No stranger shall eat the holy thing. Hired servant or sojourner — not. But the priest's bought soul or one born in his house — eat. A priest's daughter married to a stranger — not eat the heave offering. If widowed, divorced, returned home — eat as in her youth. Eaten unknowingly — restore plus a fifth. Guidance: God's altar feeds the priestly household. A daughter who marries out loses the privilege but can come home to it. Holy provision follows the lines of household, not just blood.

Offering for vow or freewill — without blemish (male of beeves, sheep, goats). Blind, broken, maimed, wen, scurvy, scabbed — not offer. Bullock or lamb superfluous or lacking — freewill yes, vow no. Bruised, crushed, broken, cut — not. Same rule for strangers. Guidance: A vow is what you promised to give; God expects the best. A freewill is what you wished to give; small irregularities are forgiven. Promise carries higher standards than spontaneity.

Bullock, sheep, or goat brought forth — seven days under its dam; the eighth day acceptable for fire offering. Cow or ewe — not kill it and her young the same day. Thanksgiving sacrifice — eaten same day, nothing till morning. Guidance: A newborn stays with its mother a week — even sacrificial animals get the bond of those first days. Don't kill mother and young the same day; the rule respects family at the altar. Thanksgiving is fresh — eaten today, never warmed over.

Keep My commandments and do them: I am the LORD. Profane not My holy name; I will be hallowed among Israel — I am the LORD who hallows you, brought you out of Egypt to be your God: I am the LORD. Guidance: The chapter closes the way Exodus closed and Leviticus keeps closing: I am the LORD who brought you out. Holiness is not earning a god; it is responding to the God who already took you home.
